factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in HVAC and industrial markets.

evidence.text: Current economic conditions are showing positive trends in each of the segments in which we participate. Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) equipment replacement, services, controls and aftermarket continue to experience strong demand. In addition, Residential and Commercial markets have seen continued momentum in the United States, positively impacting the results of our HVAC businesses. Global Industrial markets remain largely supportive of continued growth in both equipment and services.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key markets, indicating strong growth.

factors.summary: Pricing is contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the year ended December 31, 2018 increased by 10.4% , or $1,470.6 million , compared with the same period of 2017 . The components of the period change are as follows: Volume/product mix 7.3 % Acquisitions 1.1 % Pricing 1.6 % Currency translation 0.4 % Total 10.4 %

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Pricing contributes positively to revenue growth, though not the largest factor.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is positive, with both segments contributing.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the year ended December 31, 2018 increased by 10.4% , or $1,470.6 million , compared with the same period of 2017 . The components of the period change are as follows: Volume/product mix 7.3 % Acquisitions 1.1 % Pricing 1.6 % Currency translation 0.4 % Total 10.4 %

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Both segments show organic growth, supporting overall positive momentum.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing in both Climate and Industrial segments.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the year ended December 31, 2018 increased by 10.4% , or $1,470.6 million , compared with the same period of 2017 . The components of the period change are as follows: Volume/product mix 7.3 % Acquisitions 1.1 % Pricing 1.6 % Currency translation 0.4 % Total 10.4 %

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing in both segments, indicating positive trends.

factors.summary: HVAC and Industrial markets are growing fastest.

evidence.text: Current economic conditions are showing positive trends in each of the segments in which we participate. Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) equipment replacement, services, controls and aftermarket continue to experience strong demand. In addition, Residential and Commercial markets have seen continued momentum in the United States, positively impacting the results of our HVAC businesses. Global Industrial markets remain largely supportive of continued growth in both equipment and services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: HVAC and Industrial markets are growing fastest, providing strong growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding.

evidence.text: Operating margin increased to 12.2% for the year ended December 31, 2018 , compared with 11.7% for the same period of 2017 . The increase was primarily driven by higher volumes and favorable product mix (0.9%) as well as pricing improvements in excess of material inflation (0.1%). These amounts were partially offset by investments and restructuring spending (0.5%).

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by favorable factors.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by operational excellence initiatives.

evidence.text: We believe we have a solid foundation of global brands that are highly differentiated in all of our major product lines. Our growing geographic and industry diversity coupled with our large installed product base provides growth opportunities within our service, parts and replacement revenue streams. In addition, we are investing substantial resources to innovate and develop new products and services which we expect will drive our future growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements from operational initiatives are evident.

factors.summary: Management expects positive growth in both segments in 2019.

evidence.text: In 2019, we expect positive growth in both our Climate and Industrial segments, each benefiting from operational excellence initiatives, new product launches and continued productivity programs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expects positive growth in both segments, supported by growth drivers.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by increased dividends and share buybacks.

evidence.text: In June 2018, we announced an increase in our quarterly share dividend from $0.45 to $0.53 per ordinary share. This reflects an 18% increase that began with our September 2018 payment and an 83% increase since the beginning of 2016.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence is evident through significant dividend increases and buyback programs.

factors.summary: Geopolitical uncertainty in certain regions is a risk, but management remains confident.

evidence.text: While geopolitical uncertainty exists in markets such as Europe, Asia and Latin America, we are confident we will continue our strong performance globally.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Geopolitical uncertainty poses a moderate risk despite management confidence.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategy focused on growth through innovation and operational excellence.

evidence.text: To achieve our mission of being a world leader in creating comfortable, sustainable and efficient environments, we continue to focus on growth by increasing our recurring revenue stream from parts, service, controls, used equipment and rentals; and to continuously improve the efficiencies and capabilities of the products and services of our businesses. We also continue to focus on operational excellence strategies as a central theme to improving our earnings and cash flows.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: A clear growth strategy focused on innovation and operational excellence is in place.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements and economic conditions are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the year ended December 31, 2018 increased by 10.4% , or $1,470.6 million , compared with the same period of 2017 . The components of the period change are as follows: Volume/product mix 7.3 % Acquisitions 1.1 % Pricing 1.6 % Currency translation 0.4 % Total 10.4 %

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements are a sensitivity but not a dominant factor.

factors.summary: The overall economic environment is a tailwind, with positive trends across segments.

evidence.text: Current economic conditions are showing positive trends in each of the segments in which we participate. Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) equipment replacement, services, controls and aftermarket continue to experience strong demand. In addition, Residential and Commercial markets have seen continued momentum in the United States, positively impacting the results of our HVAC businesses. Global Industrial markets remain largely supportive of continued growth in both equipment and services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive economic trends across segments are driving growth.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are favorable, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the year ended December 31, 2018 increased by 10.4% , or $1,470.6 million , compared with the same period of 2017 . The components of the period change are as follows: Volume/product mix 7.3 % Acquisitions 1.1 % Pricing 1.6 % Currency translation 0.4 % Total 10.4 %

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable foreign exchange movements contribute to revenue growth.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks exist in certain regions but are not expected to hinder growth.

evidence.text: While geopolitical uncertainty exists in markets such as Europe, Asia and Latin America, we are confident we will continue our strong performance globally.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Geopolitical risks exist but are not expected to hinder growth significantly.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing, with a dividend increase and active share buyback program.

evidence.text: In June 2018, we announced an increase in our quarterly share dividend from $0.45 to $0.53 per ordinary share. This reflects an 18% increase that began with our September 2018 payment and an 83% increase since the beginning of 2016.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant increases in dividends and buybacks indicate strong shareholder returns.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are active and contributing to growth.

evidence.text: During 2018, acquisitions and equity method investments, net of cash acquired totaled $285.2 million. Related amounts in 2017 and 2016 were $157.6 million and $9.2 million, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The increasing acquisition activity and contribution to growth indicate a positive trend.
